name: Shanghai
intro: Shanghai is a natural landing page for travelers who want a modern skyline, easy metro navigation, and short urban itineraries that mix food, shopping, and architecture.
seoTitle: Shanghai Travel Guide for International Visitors
seoDescription: Explore neighborhoods, transport tips, and itinerary planning advice for a first trip to Shanghai.
tripPace: Works best as a compact city-break with room for neighborhoods and food.
highlights: A comfortable starting city for travelers who want modern transport, manageable navigation, and a softer landing into China.
Supports short itineraries well because neighborhoods and skyline experiences can be grouped cleanly.
Easy to combine with nearby scenic or slower-paced extensions like Hangzhou.

planningPriorities.title: Treat Shanghai as an urban rhythm city
planningPriorities.description: Mix skyline stops with neighborhoods, food streets, and flexible walking time instead of overpacking landmark lists.

planningPriorities.title: Choose the right neighborhood base
planningPriorities.description: The right hotel area matters more than chasing one famous address because Shanghai is best experienced by district.

planningPriorities.title: Plan nearby extensions early
planningPriorities.description: If Hangzhou or another nearby city is part of the trip, shape the rail timing before finalizing the city days.

transportNote: Shanghai is one of the easiest first cities in China for international travelers because metro use and airport transfers are comparatively straightforward.
